reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: Appointment statistics, revenue & surgeon performance
reports.earnedPendingLost: Earned · Pending · Lost breakdown per surgeon

settings.title: Settings
settings.reminderDescription: Automatically send a reminder email to patients before their scheduled appointment.

guide.pageSubtitle: Select your role below to find step-by-step instructions for your workflows.

appOwner.intro: You have full access to all areas of MedConsult. Use this guide to set up and manage your practice.
appOwner.s1i1: Navigate to Agencies in the sidebar to create or edit your agency profile.
appOwner.s1i2: Upload your logo URL, set your primary brand colour, and add your business address.
appOwner.s1i3: Set a custom sender email address so patients receive emails from your practice name.
appOwner.s1i4: Add your website URL to include it in patient-facing communications.
appOwner.s2i1: Go to Surgeons to add new consultants. Enter their name, email, specialization, and fee.
appOwner.s2i2: Each surgeon receives their own portal access to view appointments and conduct consultations.
appOwner.s2i3: Click on a surgeon's name to edit their details or view their consultation history.
appOwner.s3i1: Events represent consultation clinics or booking windows. Go to Events to create one.
appOwner.s3i2: Set the event name, venue, start and end dates, and assign a questionnaire.
appOwner.s3i3: Publish the event to make it visible on the public booking page.
appOwner.s3i4: Once closed, the event remains in the system for reporting purposes.
appOwner.s4i1: Questionnaires are pre-consultation health forms completed by patients before their appointment.
appOwner.s4i2: Go to Questionnaires to build a form with custom questions and question types.
appOwner.s4i3: Link a questionnaire to an event so it is automatically assigned to all bookings under that event.
appOwner.s5i1: The Reports page shows appointment statistics, revenue summaries, and surgeon performance.
appOwner.s5i2: Use the date filter (All Time, This Month, This Week) to narrow the reporting window.
appOwner.s5i3: Export appointment and revenue data as CSV files for offline analysis or accounting.
appOwner.s6i1: Every email sent by the system is recorded in the Email Log (accessible from the sidebar).
appOwner.s6i2: Filter by status (sent/failed), template type, and date range.
appOwner.s6i3: Export a filtered CSV for compliance audits or delivery troubleshooting.
appOwner.s7i1: Configure automatic reminder emails to patients before their scheduled appointment.
appOwner.s7i2: Set how many days in advance the reminder is sent (e.g. 2 days before).
appOwner.s7i3: Use the Email Template Preview to review all templates with realistic sample data before going live.

bookingAdmin.intro: You manage day-to-day bookings and patient relationships. Use this guide to handle consultations efficiently.
bookingAdmin.s1i1: Go to Customers to view all registered patients and their compliance status.
bookingAdmin.s1i2: Search by name, email, or nationality to find a specific patient quickly.
bookingAdmin.s1i3: Click on a patient to view their full profile, booking history, and declaration status.
bookingAdmin.s1i4: Use the bulk Send Declaration button to send the consent form to multiple patients at once.
bookingAdmin.s2i1: From a patient's profile or from Consultations, click Book Appointment.
bookingAdmin.s2i2: Select the event, surgeon, date and time slot, and set the consultation fee.
bookingAdmin.s2i3: The patient receives an automatic booking confirmation email with their appointment details.
bookingAdmin.s2i4: The assigned surgeon receives a new booking alert at the same time.
bookingAdmin.s3i1: Open the consultation and click Reschedule to choose a new date and time.
bookingAdmin.s3i2: Both the patient and the surgeon are notified automatically when a booking is rescheduled.
bookingAdmin.s3i3: To cancel, click Cancel Appointment and provide a cancellation reason.
bookingAdmin.s3i4: Cancelled appointments are kept in the system for reporting and audit purposes.
bookingAdmin.s4i1: The patient declaration is a required consent form patients must sign before their consultation.
bookingAdmin.s4i2: Send it from a patient's profile page or via the bulk send on the Customers list.
bookingAdmin.s4i3: Track signed vs unsigned status at a glance on the Customers page.
bookingAdmin.s4i4: Patients sign the declaration via a secure link in the email sent to them.
bookingAdmin.s5i1: After a consultation is completed, open it in Consultations to view the surgeon's notes.
bookingAdmin.s5i2: Attachments and media uploaded during the consultation are accessible here.
bookingAdmin.s5i3: Download a PDF summary of the consultation for patient records or referrals.

surgeon.intro: Your portal gives you access to your upcoming appointments and the tools to run consultations. This guide covers your key workflows.
surgeon.s1i1: Log in to see your personalised dashboard with today's appointments and upcoming schedule.
surgeon.s1i2: The dashboard shows each patient's name, consultation time, and declaration status.
surgeon.s1i3: Patients who have not yet signed their declaration are highlighted so you can flag this to the team.
surgeon.s2i1: Go to Appointments to see the full list of your upcoming and past consultations.
surgeon.s2i2: Each row shows the patient name, event, date/time, status, and fee.
surgeon.s2i3: Click on an appointment to open the Consultation Room.
surgeon.s3i1: Inside the Consultation Room you can view the patient's completed questionnaire answers.
surgeon.s3i2: Add your clinical notes in the Notes section — these are saved in real time.
surgeon.s3i3: Upload images, reports, or documents as attachments to the consultation record.
surgeon.s3i4: When done, mark the appointment as Complete. If the patient did not attend, mark as No-Show.
surgeon.s4i1: You can also mark an appointment as Cancelled if it cannot proceed.
surgeon.s4i2: Use the Reschedule option to propose a new date if needed — the team will be notified.
surgeon.s4i3: All status changes are logged and the patient receives an automated email notification.

customer.intro: Welcome to MedConsult. This guide explains how to book a consultation, prepare for your appointment, and use your patient portal.
customer.s1i1: Visit the events page on your clinic's website and choose an available consultation event.
customer.s1i2: Select a surgeon and an available time slot, then enter your contact details to complete the booking.
customer.s1i3: You will receive a booking confirmation email with your appointment details and a portal link.
customer.s2i1: Use the link in your confirmation email to access your secure patient portal.
customer.s2i2: Your portal shows your upcoming appointments, questionnaire, and declaration status.
customer.s2i3: You can log in at any time using the email address you registered with.
customer.s3i1: Before your appointment, you must complete a pre-consultation health questionnaire.
customer.s3i2: You will find it in your portal under your upcoming appointment.
customer.s3i3: Answer all questions honestly — this information helps your surgeon prepare for your consultation.
customer.s3i4: You can save your progress and return to complete it before your appointment date.
customer.s4i1: The patient declaration is a consent and medical information form that must be signed before your consultation.
customer.s4i2: You will receive an email with a secure link to sign it, or you can access it via your portal.
customer.s4i3: Read the document carefully before signing. If you have questions, contact the clinic directly.
customer.s4i4: Once signed, your declaration status will update automatically in the system.
customer.s5Title: On the Day of Your Appointment
customer.s5i1: Arrive on time at the venue listed in your confirmation email.
customer.s5i2: If you need to reschedule or cancel, contact the clinic as soon as possible.
customer.s5i3: After your consultation, any notes or follow-up information will be added to your portal record.
